name: BlueNoroff LaunchAgent Persistence Plist Creation
description: Detects creation of LaunchAgent or LaunchDaemon plist files with labels known to be used by BlueNoroff/Lazarus DPRK campaigns (RustBucket, BeaverTail, Hidden Risk). These plist files establish persistence by configuring macOS to execute malware payloads at login or on system boot.
query:
file where event.action == "creation" and
  file.path : ("*/Library/LaunchAgents/*.plist", "*/Library/LaunchDaemons/*.plist") and
  file.name : (
    "com.apple.systemupdate.plist",
    "com.avatar.update.wake.plist",
    "com.apple.security.updateagent.plist",
    "com.apple.Safari.helper.plist"
  )
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

name: Shell Configuration File Modification for Persistence (.zshenv)
description: Detects modifications to ~/.zshenv files which is a persistence technique used by the BlueNoroff Hidden Risk campaign (November 2024). The .zshenv file executes for every new Zsh session and critically does NOT trigger macOS Ventura background Login Items notification, making it a stealthy persistence vector.
query:
file where event.action in ("creation", "modification") and
  file.name == ".zshenv" and
  file.path : "/Users/*/.zshenv" and
  not process.executable : (
    "/bin/zsh", "/usr/bin/vim", "/usr/bin/nano",
    "/usr/local/bin/code", "/opt/homebrew/bin/*"
  )
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

name: osascript Fake Password Dialog for Credential Phishing
description: Detects execution of osascript with display dialog and hidden answer parameters, a credential phishing technique shared by AMOS Stealer, Banshee Stealer, and BlueNoroff macOS campaigns. The attacker presents a fake system dialog to trick users into entering their password.
query:
process where event.action == "exec" and
  process.name == "osascript" and
  process.command_line : "*display dialog*" and
  process.command_line : "*hidden answer*" and
  not process.parent.executable : (
    "/System/Library/CoreServices/Finder.app/*",
    "/System/Library/PreferencePanes/*",
    "/usr/libexec/SecurityAgent"
  )
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

name: macOS Keychain Credential Extraction via security CLI
description: Detects use of the macOS security command-line tool to dump or extract credentials from the Keychain, including Chrome Safe Storage key extraction. This is a common technique across macOS stealer malware families including AMOS, Banshee, and BlueNoroff campaigns.
query:
process where event.action == "exec" and
  process.name == "security" and
  process.command_line : (
    "*dump-keychain*",
    "*find-generic-password*-g*",
    "*find-internet-password*-g*",
    "*find-generic-password*-w*"
  )
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

name: Credential Validation via dscl -authonly
description: Detects use of dscl -authonly to validate captured credentials against the local directory service. After capturing a password via osascript dialog, BlueNoroff and other macOS malware validate the credential before exfiltration.
query:
process where event.action == "exec" and
  process.name == "dscl" and
  process.command_line : "*-authonly*" and
  not process.parent.executable : (
    "/System/Library/PrivateFrameworks/SystemAdministration.framework/*",
    "/usr/libexec/opendirectoryd"
  )
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

name: Cryptocurrency Wallet Extension Data Access by Non-Browser Process
description: Detects access to cryptocurrency wallet browser extension storage directories (MetaMask, Coinbase Wallet, Exodus) by non-browser processes. BlueNoroff specifically targets blockchain engineers and cryptocurrency wallet data as their primary financial theft objective.
query:
file where event.action in ("open", "read") and
  file.path : (
    "*nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn*",
    "*hnfanknocfeofbddgcijnmhnfnkdnaad*",
    "*Exodus/exodus.wallet*",
    "*Exodus/seed.seco*"
  ) and
  not process.executable : (
    "/Applications/Google Chrome.app/*",
    "/Applications/Brave Browser.app/*",
    "/Applications/Microsoft Edge.app/*",
    "/Applications/Exodus.app/*"
  )
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

name: DNS Query to linkpc.net DPRK C2 Infrastructure
description: Detects DNS queries to linkpc.net subdomains, a dynamic DNS service heavily abused by BlueNoroff/KANDYKORN campaigns for command and control infrastructure. The presence of linkpc.net DNS queries from endpoints is a high-confidence indicator of DPRK actor activity.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

name: Gatekeeper Bypass via Quarantine Attribute Removal
description: Detects removal of the com.apple.quarantine extended attribute using xattr command, which bypasses macOS Gatekeeper verification for downloaded files. This technique is used by RustBucket and other BlueNoroff payloads delivered via curl to avoid Gatekeeper inspection.
query:
process where event.action == "exec" and
  process.name == "xattr" and
  process.command_line : "*com.apple.quarantine*" and
  process.command_line : ("*-d*", "*-c*", "*--delete*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

name: TCC Protection Reset via tccutil
description: Detects use of tccutil to reset Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC) protections on macOS. Malware uses this to remove previously granted permission decisions, forcing re-prompts or bypassing access controls for AppleEvents, Full Disk Access, or other protected resources.
query:
process where event.action == "exec" and
  process.name == "tccutil" and
  process.command_line : "*reset*" and
  process.command_line : ("*AppleEvents*", "*SystemPolicyAllFiles*", "*Accessibility*", "*ScreenCapture*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

name: Archive Creation Followed by Network Exfiltration
description: Detects creation of zip archives in staging directories followed by outbound network connections, indicating data exfiltration. This matches the AMOS stealer out.zip pattern and macOS.NotLockBit S3 exfiltration behavior used in financial sector attacks.
query:
sequence by process.entity_id with maxspan=5m
  [file where event.action == "creation" and file.extension == "zip" and
    file.path : ("*/staging/*", "*/exfil/*", "*/upload/*", "*/temp/*")]
  [network where event.action == "connection_attempted" and
    destination.port in (443, 80, 8080, 8888) and
    not process.executable : ("/usr/bin/curl", "/usr/bin/wget")]
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
